content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. You can perform the following operation any number of times:


Select and remove any prefix or suffix of the array AAA, but not the entire array.
Your goal is to determine the minimum number of operations required to make KKK the most frequent element in the array AAA.

It is guaranteed that KKK is initially present in the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — denoting the length of the array AAA and the target number, which you want to make the most frequent element in the array, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations required to make KKK the most frequent number in the array.
constraints: It is guaranteed that KKK is present in the array AAA.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: : Since 111 is already the most frequent, we don't need to perform any operations.
Test case 222: : Since 222 is already the most frequent, we don't need to perform any operations.
Test case 333: : Since 111 is already the most frequent, we don't need to perform any operations.
Test case 444: :

[2,2‾,1,2]→Delete prefix from l=1 to r=2[1,2][\underline{2, 2}, 1, 2] \xrightarrow{\text{Delete prefix from } l=1 \text{ to } r=2} [1, 2][2,2​,1,2]Delete prefix from l=1 to r=2​[1,2]. Since 111 is now the most frequent, we don't need to perform any operations. The total number of operations will be 111.

Test case 555: :

[1,1‾,3,2,2]→Delete prefix from l=1 to r=2[3,2,2][\underline{1, 1}, 3, 2, 2] \xrightarrow{\text{Delete prefix from } l=1 \text{ to } r=2} [3, 2, 2][1,1​,3,2,2]Delete prefix from l=1 to r=2​[3,2,2].
[3,2,2‾]→Delete suffix from l=2 to r=3[3][3, \underline{2, 2}] \xrightarrow{\text{Delete suffix from } l=2 \text{ to } r=3} [3][3,2,2​]Delete suffix from l=2 to r=3​[3]. Since 333 is now the most frequent, we don't need to perform any operations. The total number of operations will be 222.
